title: Agent State Objects
summary: In the thread-run model, an Agent is the configured worker, a Thread is the conversation workspace, a Message is a user or assistant entry, a Run is one execution of an agent over a thread, and RunSteps are the detailed actions taken inside that run.
analogy: A thread is a case file, messages are pages added to the file, a run is assigning the case to the agent, and run steps are the timestamped actions in the case log.
diagram:
graph TD
Agent[Agent config] --> Run[Run]
Thread[Thread state] --> Run
Message[User message] --> Thread
Run --> StepA[Run step model call]
Run --> StepB[Run step tool call]
Run --> StepC[Run step final message]
body:
## First principles

First principles: long work needs a ledger. The user request, the agent decisions, tool calls, results, and final answer must be represented as durable objects so clients can retry, inspect, and audit work.

## Definition

In the thread-run model, an Agent is the configured worker, a Thread is the conversation workspace, a Message is a user or assistant entry, a Run is one execution of an agent over a thread, and RunSteps are the detailed actions taken inside that run.

## How it fits together

The object model separates state from execution. You can add messages to a thread, start a run, poll until the run is complete or needs action, then list messages and run steps to understand the result.

## Worked example

```python
# pip install azure-ai-projects azure-ai-agents>=1.2.0b3 azure-identity --pre
# This shape mirrors Azure SDK samples that expose threads, messages, runs, and run steps.
import os, time
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectClient

project_client = AIProjectClient(
    endpoint=os.environ["PROJECT_ENDPOINT"],
    credential=DefaultAzureCredential(),
)

with project_client:
    agents = project_client.agents
    agent = agents.create_agent(
        model=os.environ["MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME"],
        name="state-objects-demo",
        instructions="Answer in one paragraph and cite which tool you used if any.",
    )
    thread = agents.threads.create()
    agents.messages.create(thread_id=thread.id, role="user", content="Explain what a run step is.")
    run = agents.runs.create(thread_id=thread.id, agent_id=agent.id)

    while run.status in {"queued", "in_progress", "requires_action"}:
        time.sleep(1)
        run = agents.runs.get(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)

    for step in agents.run_steps.list(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id):
        print(step.get("id"), step.get("status"), step.get("type"))
    for message in agents.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id):
        print(message.role, message.text_messages[-1].text.value if message.text_messages else "")
    agents.delete_agent(agent.id)
```

*Minimal thread run loop using the azure-ai-agents sample surface*

drills.q: What object represents one execution attempt?
Where do you inspect tool calls after execution?

teachItBack: The state model is a ledger for agent work. Messages record conversation content, runs record execution, and run steps expose how the result was produced. This is why agents are easier to audit than a pile of raw model calls.

glossary.definition: One agent execution over a thread.
A granular action inside a run, such as a model step or tool call.

sources.title: Azure SDK for Python: MCP create and process sample
Microsoft Learn: Foundry quickstart with Azure AI Projects 2.x

quiz.q: Why should allowed_tools be set on an MCP attachment?
choices: To enforce least privilege by limiting which MCP server tools the agent may call
quiz.explanation: allowed_tools narrows authority to approved tool names.
